steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that means a specific point in time

steps.m: when (referring to something that will definitely happen in the future - the earth's shaking)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it specifically about future events that will certainly happen. Here it introduces what will happen when the earth is shaken (زُلْزِلَتِ).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Passive form shows something else is doing the shaking

steps.m: she was shaken repeatedly (referring to الْأَرْضُ/the earth)
steps.n: Feminine past tense marker shows the earth (feminine) is the subject
Basic noun form with vowel marks added to show how to say it correctly

steps.m: the earth (subject of زُلْزِلَتِ - was shaken)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject being shaken in the verse
Added vowels to the root letters to make a four-letter verb meaning 'he shook something back and forth'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the event itself

steps.m: its violent shaking (referring to Earth's shaking)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this is Earth's shaking, since Earth (الأرض) is mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to make something do the action' instead of just doing it
The تِ at the end shows that 'she' (the Earth) did the action. The Earth (الْأَرْضُ) is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: and she made (something) come out (subject: الْأَرْضُ)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this action to what came before it
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make it mean the actual earth/ground we walk on
Added ٱلْ to make it specific - this is talking about THE earth, not just any earth/land
The damma at the end shows this is the subject doing the action of bringing forth in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'became heavy' (verb) to 'burdens' (plural noun) - like going from 'weighed' to 'weights'

steps.m: her burdens (referring to the earth's burdens)
steps.n: Adds feminine possessive pronoun هَا referring to الأرض (the earth) which is feminine in Arabic. This word is the direct object of أَخْرَجَتِ (she brought forth), showing what the earth expelled.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to ٱلْإِنسٰنُ (the human/man) which is its subject.
Root meaning 'being social' becomes a noun for all humans as a group
Changes from general humankind to referring to an individual human
Adds the definite article to specify a particular human

steps.m: the human being (as the subject who speaks)
steps.n: Adds the nominative case ending because this word is the subject of قَالَ (he said) in this ayah
Basic interrogative particle used to ask questions. In this ayah, it's asking 'what is wrong with it?' about something feminine.
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Basic preposition showing purpose or direction
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to next part

steps.m: for her/it (referring to the human's statement in إِنسٰنُ)
steps.n: Added ها ('her/it') to show what the 'for' is referring to - the statement or situation being talked about
The root letters form a basic noun meaning 'day'. This word shows when the earth tells its news (تُحَدِّثُ).

steps.m: on that specific day (when تُحَدِّثُ happens)
steps.n: Adding ئِذٍ makes it refer specifically to the Day of Judgment that's being discussed, when the earth will tell its news

steps.m: to tell or speak about something new

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter د shows doing something with more emphasis or repeatedly
تُ at start shows it's happening now (present) and 'she' is doing it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form meaning 'news'
Added أ at the beginning and ا after second letter to make it plural, with sukun on second letter

steps.m: its news (referring to Earth's news in يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show it's the Earth's news being told. This word is what's being told (object) in the phrase 'that Day it (Earth) will tell its news'
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English. Here it connects to the word أَوْحَىٰ (revealed).

steps.m: because/due to the fact that (connects to أَوْحَىٰ - revealed)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the start changes the meaning to show reason or cause, like saying 'because' or 'due to' in English. It explains why something happened.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the nurturing quality
Added كَ at the end to show possession (your). The word is the object of بِأَنَّ (that/because), which makes it take a fatha at the end.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to be revealed (to لَهَا)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'caused to reveal' instead of just 'revealed'. Connected to لَهَا (to her/it) in the verse
This is a basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'. It connects to the verb أَوْحَىٰ (inspired/revealed) to show who received the inspiration.
هَا is added to mean 'her' or 'it' (when referring to a feminine thing). In this ayah, it refers to the earth receiving Allah's inspiration/revelation.
Connected the root letters together to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha to show this word is being described by what follows

steps.m: on that day (when يَصْدُرُ ٱلنَّاسُ - people will emerge)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that specific day' being referenced in the context. This refers to the day when people will emerge, as mentioned in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he emerges/comes forth (subject: ٱلنَّاسُ - the people)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes what the people (ٱلنَّاسُ) will do on that day.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be social' into noun meaning 'people'

steps.m: the people (subject of يَصْدُرُ - they emerge)
steps.n: Added ال to show we're talking about specific people, not just any people. The ن gets a shadda (doubled) after ال.

steps.m: the people (subject of يَصْدُرُ - they emerge)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence - the people are the ones emerging.

steps.m: to separate or break apart into pieces

steps.n: Added shadda (double letter) and fatha marks to make the root into 'he separated'
Added alif and ات to make it into a plural noun meaning separate groups
Added hamza prefix to emphasize the state of being scattered

steps.m: in scattered groups (describes how الناس/the people will come forth)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this word describes how something happens (like saying 'quickly' or 'separately')
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters 'r-'-y' into 'he saw' in past tense
Changed from 'he saw' to 'is seen/shown' by making it passive - added يُ at start and changed middle vowel
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something happens
Added shadda on ل for emphasis

steps.m: so that they may be shown (their deeds)
steps.n: Added وْا to make it plural - now it refers to multiple people being shown their deeds
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed verb into a noun meaning one single action or deed
Changed single deed into multiple deeds using the plural pattern أَفْعَال
Changed alif to alif with overbar (purely spelling variation)

steps.m: their deeds (object of لِّيُرَوْا۟ - so they may see)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their deeds. This word is what they will see (object of the verb لِّيُرَوْا۟)
This is a question word that became a conditional word, used to refer to any person who might do the action. It connects to the verb يَعْمَلْ (does/works) that comes after it.
The فَ prefix shows this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before. It connects to يَعْمَلْ (does/works) to mean 'then whoever does...'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with sukun and damma markings to show basic present tense form

steps.m: he does/will do (in response to فَمَن - so whoever)
steps.n: Changed the final damma to sukun because it follows 'whoever' (فَمَن) in a conditional statement
Basic concept of weight or heaviness
Added vowels to make a past tense verb about becoming heavy

steps.m: a measure of weight (used to measure ذَرَّةٍ/atom's weight)
steps.n: Changed from 'heavy' verb to noun meaning 'weight measure' by adding مِ at start and changing middle vowels. Used here to measure how heavy an atom is.

steps.m: a measure of weight (object being measured)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of يَعْمَلْ (does/works)
Basic meaning of scattering tiny things
Doubling the middle letter intensifies the meaning to refer to the smallest possible particle
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific tiny particle
The -in ending shows this tiny particle is being measured or weighed (connected to مِثْقَالَ meaning 'weight of')
Basic concept of goodness or choice
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make it mean 'a good thing' instead of just the general idea of 'good'
Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's what is being done in يَعْمَلْ (does/works).

steps.m: to see/perceive with eyes or mind
steps.n: Basic concept of seeing or perceiving

steps.m: he sees/will see (verb connected to مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ)
steps.n: Added يَ prefix to show 'he' is doing the action in present/future. The أ and ي from root dropped because they're weak letters that change in certain forms.

steps.m: he will see it (referring to مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what is being seen - refers back to the atom's weight of good mentioned earlier
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to unspecified people
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَعْمَلْ (does/works) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb

steps.m: he does/will do (jussive mood because it follows مَن)
steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present/future tense. The sukun at the end (jussive mood) is because it comes after مَن (whoever) in the ayah.
Basic concept of weight or heaviness
Added vowels to make a basic verb showing something becoming heavy

steps.m: a measure of weight (used to measure ذَرَّةٍ - a tiny speck)
steps.n: Changed from 'became heavy' (ثَقُلَ) to 'weight measure' (مِثْقَال) by adding مِ at start and changing the middle vowels. Like turning 'heavy' into 'scale'.

steps.m: a measure of weight (object being measured)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَعْمَلْ (does/works)
Basic meaning of scattering or spreading
Doubling of middle radical to intensify meaning
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific tiny particle
The kasra tanween shows this is being acted upon by the word مِثْقَالَ (weight of)
Basic concept of evil or badness
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling) on ر makes the evil more intense
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of 'doing' in the phrase 'whoever does evil'
Basic meaning of seeing or observing
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he saw' from the root meaning 'to see'
Added يَ at the beginning to show present/future tense. The hamza (أ) drops out because it's between two vowels, which is a common rule in Arabic.

steps.m: he will see it (referring to the tiny deed mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show what is being seen. This refers back to the tiny deed (مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse. The final ى is dropped before adding هُۥ.
